SEARCH
Package dev.jeka.core.api.java

Class JkJarWriter

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.java.JkJarWriter
public class JkJarWriter extends Object
Writes JAR content, ensuring valid directory entries are always created and duplicated items are ignored.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Close the writer.
    static JkJarWriter
    of(Path target)
    Create a new JkJarWriter instance.
    void
    writeEntries(Path dir, PathMatcher matcher)
    Write entries from the specific directories and matching the specified matcher.
    void
    writeEntries(JarFile jarFile)
    Write all entries from the specified jar file.
    void
    writeEntry(String entryName, InputStream inputStream)
    Writes an entry.
    void

    Write the specified manifest.

  • Method Details

    • of

      public static JkJarWriter of(Path target)
      Create a new JkJarWriter instance.
      Parameters:
      target - the file to write
      Throws:
      UncheckedIOException - if the file cannot be opened or if the file cannot be found

    • writeManifest

      public void writeManifest(Manifest manifest)
      Write the specified manifest.
      Parameters:
      manifest - the manifest to write
      Throws:
      UncheckedIOException - of the manifest cannot be written

    • writeEntries

      public void writeEntries(JarFile jarFile)
      Write all entries from the specified jar file.
      Parameters:
      jarFile - the source jar file
      Throws:
      UncheckedIOException - if the entries cannot be written

    • writeEntries

      public void writeEntries(Path dir, PathMatcher matcher)
      Write entries from the specific directories and matching the specified matcher.

    • writeEntry

      public void writeEntry(String entryName, InputStream inputStream)
      Writes an entry. The inputStream is closed once the entry has been written
      Parameters:
      entryName - The name of the entry
      inputStream - The stream from which the entry's data can be read
      Throws:
      UncheckedIOException - if the write fails

    • close

      public void close()
      Close the writer.
      Throws:
      UncheckedIOException - if the file cannot be closed
